title: Geopolitical Spillover: Assessing the Impact of Iran-Israel Tensions on the Tanker Shipping Industry
section_path: Geopolitical Spillover: Assessing the Impact of Iran-Israel Tensions on the Tanker Shipping Industry
section_path_text: Geopolitical Spillover: Assessing the Impact of Iran-Israel Tensions on the Tanker Shipping Industry
summary:
Main
Image reference: 685135f0197fc2a6b57a74ae_Strait of Hormuz.png | assets/2025-06-18_geopolitical-spillover-assessing-the-impact-of-iran-israel-tensions-on-the-tanke_img_685135f0197fc2a6b57a74ae-straitofhor_27237613dc77.png

Executive Summary:
Escalating tensions between Iran and Israel are analysed for their potential effects on global energy markets, particularly concerning the Strait of Hormuz. While historical data indicates a full closure by Iran is improbable, disruptions via attacks, mines, or vessel harassment could significantly affect oil prices, shipping routes, and freight rates. Given China's position as Iran's primary oil buyer and strategic ally, a sustained closure would negatively impact Iran's revenue.

children.summary: Image reference: 685135f0197fc2a6b57a74ae_Strait of Hormuz.png | assets/2025-06-18_geopolitical-spillover-assessing-the-impact-of-iran-israel-tensions-on-the-tanke_img_685135f0197fc2a6b57a74ae-straitofhor_27237613dc77.png

children.title: Executive Summary:
children.summary: Escalating tensions between Iran and Israel are analysed for their potential effects on global energy markets, particularly concerning the Strait of Hormuz. While historical data indicates a full closure by Iran is improbable, disruptions via attacks, mines, or vessel harassment could significantly affect oil prices, shipping routes, and freight rates.

children.title: 1. Strait of Hormuz: Strategic Context
children.summary: The Strait of Hormuz is one of the world's most critical maritime chokepoints and plays a central role in global energy trade as it connects the Persian Gulf to the Gulf of Oman and the Indian Ocean. In 2023, an estimated 20.9 million barrels per day of petroleum liquids transited through the strait, according to the U.S.

children.title: Risk Overview
children.summary: During the Iran-Iraq War (1980-1988), particularly in the so-called "Tanker War" phase, both nations targeted oil tankers transiting the Gulf; yet the Strait itself was never fully closed. and its allies intensified naval escorts and mine-sweeping operations to ensure the uninterrupted flow of oil, underscoring the global imperative to maintain the route's accessibility.

children.title: 2. Port Infrastructure & Freight Market Impacts
children.summary: Recent developments triggered a sharp surge in oil prices, with Brent crude rising from the mid-$60s to the mid-$70s as of Friday, June 13, reinforcing the upward momentum that had begun earlier in the week. This spike echoed the market reaction during the early stages of the Russia-Ukraine conflict when oil prices climbed even higher due to disruption risks tied to Russia's role at the time as the world's third-largest oil producer.

children.title: Elevated Risk to Iranian Oil Port Infrastructure
section_path: Elevated Risk to Iranian Oil Port Infrastructure
children.section_path_text: Elevated Risk to Iranian Oil Port Infrastructure
children.summary: Reports indicate that Israeli strikes targeted defenses near Iran's key port of Bandar Abbas. Although Iran maintains that operations remain unaffected, any significant damage to this strategically located port, adjacent to the Strait of Hormuz, could disrupt oil vessel traffic.

children.title: Freight Market - Vessel count in the AG (VLCC)
section_path: Freight Market - Vessel count in the AG (VLCC)
children.section_path_text: Freight Market - Vessel count in the AG (VLCC)
children.summary:
We have started seeing a reduction in the vessel count at the load area of AG for the VLCC TD3C route, although the WS direction upward trend is not yet significantly pronounced. children.title: Scenario A: Full Closure of the Strait
section_path: Scenario A: Full Closure of the Strait
children.section_path_text: Scenario A: Full Closure of the Strait
children.summary: Closure of the Strait of Hormuz could trigger oil price spikes exceeding $100 per barrel, leading to global economic instability and a higher risk of wider conflict. Even countries not directly importing Gulf oil would be affected due to an immediate drop in global supply, driving up prices across the supply chain.

children.title: FFA Market Signals a Sharp Rise amid Middle East Tensions
section_path: FFA Market Signals a Sharp Rise amid Middle East Tensions
children.section_path_text: FFA Market Signals a Sharp Rise amid Middle East Tensions
children.summary:
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-06-18 141507.png | assets/2025-06-18_geopolitical-spillover-assessing-the-impact-of-iran-israel-tensions-on-the-tanke_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_77d07caf6186.png
https://app.signalocean.com/tanker/dynamic/market-prices-tankers-downloadable
‍
Forward Freight Agreement (FFA) market analysts are projecting a significant increase in VLCC earnings on the Middle East Gulf to China route, with July rates expected to exceed $40,000 per day. These FFA projections reflect growing uncertainty and bullish sentiment in the tanker segment as geopolitical tensions escalate.

children.title: Scenario B: Partial Disruption
children.summary: In a scenario where shipping passage disruptions occur, Iran could implement measures such as attacks or hijacking, along with overall strategies to impede oil vessel movements. Iran is also expected to utilize the Houthis' increasing attacks against Red Sea shipping activities, although the Houthis' strength remains uncertain following U.S.

children.title: 4. Asian Oil Consumption: Why a Long-Term Closure Scenario is Considered Not Strategic for the Iranian Oil Revenue Industry
section_path: 4. Asian Oil Consumption: Why a Long-Term Closure Scenario is Considered Not Strategic for the Iranian Oil Revenue Industry
children.section_path_text: 4. Asian Oil Consumption: Why a Long-Term Closure Scenario is Considered Not Strategic for the Iranian Oil Revenue Industry
children.summary: Iran, a significant global oil exporter, primarily sends its oil to China, a crucial partner in Asia. Notably, Iranian oil sales to China saw a rise in mid-March, preceding increased U.S.

children.title: 5. Bypassing the Strait: Strategic Alternatives and Pipeline Options
section_path: 5. Bypassing the Strait: Strategic Alternatives and Pipeline Options
children.section_path_text: 5. Bypassing the Strait: Strategic Alternatives and Pipeline Options
children.summary: The United States has, in recent years, become far less dependent on oil from the Persian Gulf because of the rise of fracking and other advanced techniques to extract oil. However, the closure of the Strait, as analyzed above, would be a significant blow for China, which still imports large quantities of oil from the region.

children.title: Strategic reserves
children.summary: The International Energy Agency is closely monitoring the impacts on oil markets of the Israel-Iran situation. The oil market is currently well-supplied, with non-OECD+ supply forecast to increase by 1.3 mb/d this year, outpacing expected global demand growth of around 700 kb/d.

children.title: Maritime risk security awareness
children.summary: BIMCO has advised ship owners to implement ship defense measures outlined in industry documents , report suspicious sightings to the UK's Maritime Trade Operations, reconsider current routing, and keep seafarer safety in mind, which are among the recommendations it has shared with clients. Larsen said any perception of the United States' involvement will bring greater risks for ships, though so far the U.S.

children.title: Conclusion
children.summary: Early reports confirmed escalating tensions, with Israel facing intense Iranian missile strikes, while the U.S. urges restraint and pushes for de-escalation to prevent a broader regional conflict.
